definitions: An angle that is not perpendicular or parallel to a given plane or line; an oblique or tilted angle.
A deliberate tilt or slant given to an object or surface, often for aesthetic or functional reasons.
examples: The roof had a canted angle to shed water more effectively.
He adjusted the canted angle of the camera for a dramatic shot.
The architect designed the wall with a slight canted angle.
The canted angle of the display made it easier to read.
